concerns.text: FR-007 ('sensitivity analysis by re-running... excluding participants who failed the manipulation check') anchors to US-3. However, the logic for handling manipulation check failures (flagging, exclusion criteria) is defined in US-2 (Data Collection). While US-3 covers the *analysis* step, the requirement to *exclude* based on a flag generated in US-2 creates a weak traceability link. Consider anchoring FR-007 to US-2 or explicitly stating it serves the data integrity goal of US-2 as well as the analysis goal of US-3.

SC-002 ('statistical power... measured against target sample size N=300') anchors to FR-004. However, FR-004 only mandates the *execution* of t-tests. It does not mandate a *power calculation* or the *targeting* of a sample size. The requirement to ensure 80% power for N=300 is a design constraint not explicitly captured in the Functional Requirements list. A new FR (e.g., FR-009) or an amendment to FR-002 (randomization/assignment) is needed to explicitly state the system must support or enforce the N=300 target to satisfy SC-002.
